review_text: We were here for the great ocean Road running festival. The location was ideal. The front desktop was efficient and welcoming. All amenities were spotlessly, clean and very accessible. Highly recommend.
The place had everything: top-notch facilities for a caravan park, safe, clean, and with everything you need to have a great time.

Upon arrival at 4.15pm, office was closed for 15 minutes.  Another family was also left waiting for 15 minutes with no one in attendance to check-in.  Unfortunately the cutlery in drawer was dirty and carpet in main sitting area was heavily marked (extremely soiled).  Small, old fashioned round soaps provided together with 1 ply toilet paper.  Expected more for $240 night especially compared to what other G'day Parks provide (shampoo, conditioner, liquid bodysoap and higher grade loo paper).  No park information pack provided.

Had a bit of a hicup when we got to Apolo Bay as the other park we were going to be staying had booked us for a motor home when I had booked for a camper trailer.
So we had to find somewhere in a hurry to go to. We called Apolo Bay Holiday Park spoke to Bruce, he was so accomadating and was able to give us a powered camp site in a quiet area in the park. We stayed here for about 7 days in our camper trailer . The piece and tranquillity was amazing . Excellent facilities all round .
Bathroom areas so Excellent so much room in the shower area I was in shock how excellentit was . I had never see  such brilliant bathroom facilities with so much room in the shower area to get ready before and after your shower . Toilet area awsome also, nothing im that bathroom was cramped at all .The floor didn't get wet at all I take mats to stand on all the time as most shower floors get so wet it's impossible to dry your feet properly . Even without mats these floors would not be like a river in the bathroom .
Laundry facilities great also . Lots of line space to hang clothes.
Camp kitchen excellent and games area excellent.
Very impressed all round with this park .
Thanks so much for all your help Bruce we really did appreciate it so much .
We will be back another time .
Regards Kellie and Bernie.
Great cabins (dated but so clean).  Kitchen items also spotless.  Could have done with a few more power points.  Otherwise a very comfortable stay 15 min walk from restaurants.
Expensive
Nice little park. Super clean and tidy and close to town

Stayed for 3 nights
Beautiful campsite. Well maintained, clean facilities.
Very reasonable rates.
Close to beach, shops etc.

Great little holiday park, pitch was perfect for our large camper, bathroom facilities spotless and a great equipment for cooking and play area for the kids. Ten minute walk into town that had some cracking restaurants
Good location. Clean & quiet place to stay

Stayed 3 nights, did not get the cabin we booked, we booked a queen cabin which sleeps 2 and has a balcony.  Close to beach and town.  We got a cabin that sleeps 4 without any balcony.  To sit outside we had to sit in car park between our car and a motorhome behind.  Bathroom tap leaked(sink stained), vanity had water damage.  Very tired looking also needed paint.  One wall in living area looked like it was patched by a child.  Kitchen tap leaked, only supplied very small milk and 3 coffees, tea bags and sugars.  One bath towel each for 3 night stay.  Bed was very uncomfortable and felt like it was going to collapse each time we moved.  Fly screen on front door, kitchen and spare bedroom all damaged.  Park was well equipped and clean.  Sign in cabin advising we had to clean before leaving otherwise a fee would apply, since when has the guest had to clean, usually these places employ cleaners for this.  TV in lounge had no signal, we had to do a Channel scan to get tv, tv in bedroom had reception issue couldn’t watch.  Lady at the reception was very nice and pleasant.  This accommodation was well over priced for what we had, especially since we didn’t get what we booked.  Unfortunately we won’t be staying there again.

We recently stayed here and unfortunately had a very disappointing experience from the moment we arrived.
At check-in we were told we owed an additional $20 to have a bed made up for our child, after booking and paying for 3 people. This was never stated on our Booking.com confirmation, and we did not receive any email advising of extra linen charges. When we raised this, the staff member was dismissive and insisted it must have been in an email from Booking.com. We checked, it wasn’t.
The room itself was extremely small and poorly presented. The bunk room was barely large enough to fit three narrow bunks, let alone a 14-year-old comfortably. It honestly felt like three beds had been squeezed into a space far too small for them.
What disappointed us most was the attitude at reception. Instead of acknowledging the confusion around the extra charge, we were met with rudeness and zero willingness to help.
We understand budget accommodation comes with compromises, but transparency, basic comfort, and respectful customer service should still be expected.
Unfortunately, we would not stay here again
Rude staff, ignorant of their own mistakes, forgot to send us the email about the extra bedding fee (illegal in victoria) and kids beds were cramped with vulgar graffiti written under them.

Great location. Great amenities.  Cooking, cleaning etc. Good clean pool. Wheelchair friendly.  Good kids playground and games relax room. Camp sites are flat and well maintained.  Rated 4 star as thought the sites a little cramped. Overall a very pleasant stay, friendly staff. Good value for money.

We stayed at a site in our campervan.
We’ve been travelling all over and this park was amazing! - very clean, pretty, spacious. Great great value for money.

The park is close to the town of Apollo Bay...so many really nice places to dine✅ park is so well presented and amenities are really clean. We stayed on a powered site near the pool and playground. Park Had a fantastic camp kitchen with two bbqs internal and a pizza oven out on the covered verandah. Nice place to sit and watch kids play on the equipment while tea is cooking 🤗 not a long walk to the beach for a swim.
Booked in last minute. Placed between the toilets and the child games open till midnight. Very expensive and a unfriendly welcome

Great spot for a family holiday with young kids under 5. Roomy cabins, comfortable and plenty of deck space to sit, read and eat. Easy walk to the beach (great vantage point for NYE fireworks) and a slightly longer walk (or short drive) to the main strip.
Kids can comfortably run around/ride scooters safely even with cars infrequently coming in and out. Generally a very quiet spot. Great place to stay.

Food wise
Apollo Bay Bakery: amazing good for brekky/lunch/snacks and top notch coffee. Pies are fantastic.
George's: best food for families with young kids who have chips with every second meal. Big variety, and the food is fantastic. Better souvlaki then alot of spots in Melbourne.

Terrible experience. Reservation staff double booked our cabin.
Cleaning staff let themselves in on us mid- breakfast. Manager was very unprofessional and unhelpful. We showed our invoice of checkout day being Christmas Day after rudely being accused we had made the mistake and mixed up our dates. No apology, just abruptly demanded to leave before the next guests arrive. No compensation for ruining our Christmas Day.
Mattresses very hard. Next to no wifi. Useless tv unless you watch outdated DVD’s, no tea-towels etc for kitchenette and minimally stocked. Shower curtain and very small shower with no where to put soap or shampoo bottles. Only one bar to hand a towel inside after showering. Very basic accomodation.

A very basic cabin. No tea-towels or anything like that for kitchenette. Mattress uncomfortable and very firm. Terrible wifi, almost non-existent. Staff quite rude, ran as a business only with the feeling of get in, pay, get out. |Our cabin was double booked and we were abruptly quicked out mid breakfast on Christmas Eve. There was no apology, no compensation for ruining our Christmas plans and much looked forward to holiday. We should have been able to stay and double booking issue resolved by management rather than kicking us out to save face and resolve their incompetence. After the rudeness of the owner, I wanted to leave despite my Christmas Day being ruined as it left us with a very bad feeling. Totally unprofessional.

Quiet surprised by this park. I don’t think the website does it justice. Everything is so clean and organised, as well as well presented.
The camp kitchen and Facilties are second to none. There is a fully enclosed clean kitchen with tables and chairs. Upstairs is a lounge and tv / foxtel Facilties. Close to the township. Walking distance for those long lunches.
One of two camp parks close walk to town

We stayed at the Apollo Bay Holiday Park over the Melbourne Cup long weekend. The lady at reception was very friendly and helpful. The park was fully booked, it was very neat and clean. The amenities were modern, very clean. Many families enjoyed the pool area. I highly recommend this superb Holiday Park.

Location was pretty good but it was extremely overpriced for what it was. Paid $55 for a powered site which had an apartment building directly behind it, the heated pool was not very heated (this was told at time of booking), the amenities were clean, camp kitchen was great but didnt have any plates, cutlery pans etc which was super annoying and wifi didnt work. Wouldnt stay here again sadly.
A superb site, we parked up with our campervan for one night.  So clean with great facilities, would have stayed longer if we could have.  Would highly recommend

Nice facilities
Spacious showers
Nice sites
Indifferent management

Close enough to town and the beach and the site was clean but we found a few maintenance issues with our cabin. The heater banged when the thermostat cut in and out so we had to turn it off at night also, when you turned on the hot water in the shower the pipes rattled so much you could feel the whole cabin shaking. We had a superior cabin and the only difference between that and a basic cabin seemed to be that it had a queen bed and was slightly bigger in size. Other than that everything else seemed the same. We needed our towels replaced as they were wet after 2 days, but when my wife asked she was told that she needed to wash them and dry them herself. Eventually we got the towels replaced. Parking was tight and the photo advertised shows an executive cabin so l would suggest looking at the plans carefully before purchasing a cabin. Having been in a lot of cabins at other  caravan parks this is the worst we have been at a wont be going back. l'll be looking at Big4 for a change. l would rate it 3.5 if l could..

Apollo Bay Holiday Park is very central, a G'Day Park, the reasons why we chose it.|It is very family-oriented, with lots of facilities on offer and lots for the family to do.|Our cabin was good, clean and comfortable.|The Manager was friendly - thank you.|We had a great stay.|Yes, well recommended !|Thank you.

Fantastic! Beautiful accommodation and lovely staff.

Highly recommended!
无敌海景，设施齐全，服务周到。
Clean and friendly service. Large and comfortable rooms. Washing facilities are really good.

Great spot to stay in, all the facilities are kept very clean, plus it's so close to the beaches and town centre.

Staff were also very helpful. Worth the visit

Nice, very clean park, good sites lovely camp kitchen, just disappointed that you had to pay for heating and BBQ within this facility. Would expect that cooking and heating facilities would be included in site fees as per the norm.
It was very nice and quiet the time we visited
Very nice people and well maintained park

Had a lovely stay at the park, very clean and upkeep spotless. Had a cabin opposite the pool although we didn't use it because it was winter. The shower cubicles were fantastic. The cabin had everything you could want. Had a great stay.

12 out of 10 shout out for Jenny .. I called to cancel our van site booking when let down by the person we were hiring from  .. our trip on The Great Ocean Road was in tatters .. Jenny took my call .. she was so sympathetic and went straight into problem solve mode .. immediately offering suggestions and alternatives.. we eventually settled on the beaut deluxe ocean view cabin .. Jenny was there to welcome us on arrival and all the stress dissipated .. she even gave us marshmallows and sticks for toasting!  Gold medal Jenny and all our gratitude!
Cheryl and Alan

Ladies showers were clean but hot water temp not that hot, could be hotter. $1 coin to use their bbq and also $1 to use the gas heater which lasted 30 minutes. No oven just a stove.
No boom gate so be sure to lock or pack away valuables.

Walking distance to town was great.  Park was clean but you had to pay extra for everything.  You had to pay for the bbq, pay for the heater which only lasted 30 minutes.  We went to watch a movie in the games room but there was no remote for the TV.  When we asked about it the man was rude.  When we were discussing how we were going to park the man yelled out that he's been there 20 years just park the way he said.  Big 4 might be the better option.

Beware- false advertising on site. Booked a superior cabin which is advertised to sleep 6 and have a queen bed in the main bedroom. When arrived the cabin slept 4 and had a double bed in main room. When I brought this up I was told it was because I booked through booking.com - which I didn't. I booked directly through their website. The excuse given was that two of the superior cabins are like this...which is not what I booked or what it states on the website.
Gave two stars as I was able to get a full refund without any issues. Just caused a lot of stress trying to find a better cabin on a busy long weekend.
Very neat park ... & a very obliging owner... thanks
Great place to stay.  Cabins very comfortable and clean

Clean park, very expensive. We requested a tree free site when we booked. Arrived a week later to be given a site that should be, in my opinion, for motorhomes and had a tree over it. When I advised it was unsuitable, I was told to Take it or Leave It. I asked where would I park my car and was told not to put it on grass and it is to go on the road in front of the van. Which is the only road in and out on that side.
After parking, I wandered through the park to discover plenty of van parks available behind the amenities block.
Worst experience ever. Staff argumentative sites small ( nowhere to park cars ) not aloud to park near grass everything in camp Kitchen costs extra. Very expensive rates . I wouldn’t recommend .

Close enough to town and the beach, but we found a few maintenance issues with our cabin, and it's stingy when it comes to little extras we are used to getting in other caravan parks. Things like shampoo, conditioner, decent water jug, and sufficient coffee for our stay don't exist here.

Klein, aber fein. Wir haben spontan angehalten und nach einem Platz für unseren Motorhome gefragt. War gar kein Problem. Spielplatz und Pool quasi direkt vor der Tür. Toiletten und Duschen sehr modern.
Very pricey, crammed in like a car park and the amenities haven’t been properly cleaned for a long time.  Non existent WiFi despite what they tell you when you check in (I would go elsewhere though)
We’ve been staying here every time we visit the west coast since 2012 and everything is always well maintained and clean, we can’t recommend this place highly enough.

Most expensive of 22 parks in our 2 months of travel from Port Lincoln to Foster Tuncurry .
Sites weren’t wide enough to put out your awning ,jammed in like sardines.
Everything about this Holiday park. Is absolutely fantastic xo. The staff are very kind.
Nice clean cabin.  Close to shops and beach
We had a spa cabin and were SO happy with how spacious and clean it was. The staff were friendly and helpful too. Our best stay so far on the Great Ocean Road, (after a couple of mediocre ones).

As advertised, clean and good walking distance from town.
Unclear from the website that bedding would not be included for all beds. The bedding that was included wasn’t particularly nice in texture or warmth.
Amazing well kept caravan park with excellent clean facilities and friendly helpful staff. 1st time in Apollo bay and would love to come and stay again.
The man In reception was so rude and arrogant on arrival we left early very disappointed as we have always stayed at the gday parks
This is our 4th time stay here and we love this place that's why we keep coming back... great place to rest and relax! Kids have so much fun in pool and playground. 100% would recommend over and over

Great customer service!
Bruce is a very nice guy, kind and very friendly !

Memorable Stay at Apollo Bay Holiday Park
1. First Impressions and Overall Experience:
This was my first visit to Apollo Bay Holiday Park, and I stayed here with my wife during a holiday trip. We thoroughly enjoyed our one-day stay, although we wished we had more time to explore everything the park has to offer. The premises are filled with recreational activities, making it a perfect choice for families or groups looking to unwind and enjoy their holiday.

2. Accommodation and Amenities
We opted for a budget-friendly cabin, and it exceeded our expectations. The accommodation was spacious and equipped to comfortably host up to six people, featuring one master bed and four bunk beds. For those who enjoy cooking during their trips, the cabin came fully stocked with all the necessary kitchen essentials, making meal preparation hassle-free.
3. Service and Hospitality
The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful throughout our stay. In particular, the lady who assisted us during check-in was exceptionally friendly and made the process smooth and stress-free. The service we received was truly commendable and added to the positive experience.
4. Nearby Attractions
The Apollo Bay Beach, located just a short distance away, is undoubtedly one of the major highlights of this location. The beach is stunning, offering plenty of opportunities to relax and soak in the beauty of the coast. It’s a place where you could easily spend hours and still feel like it’s not enough.
5. Final Recommendation
For anyone planning a trip to Apollo Bay, I strongly recommend staying at Apollo Bay Holiday Park. Whether you’re traveling as a couple, with family, or in a group, this holiday park provides everything you need for a comfortable and enjoyable stay. With its holiday vibes, great amenities, and excellent location, it’s a place worth experiencing.

Super tidy park.  Great amenities, very modern.
Can see why this park has won awards

Park grounds absolutely spotless and very well maintained with a really welcoming feel.
There was a mix up in our cabin that caught us off guard, however, Bruce was absolutely outstanding in rectifying the situation seamlessly.
We had the best time!
The amenities are great, the games room was retro ( next time we’ll bring more coins) 🤣
Laundry well fitted out.
Communal shower blocks clean and tidy.
General ambiance was relaxed and fun.
We’re from WA and will definitely be coming back next year!!
Thanks to Bruce for being such a great host!
✨🥂☀️🌊🏓🛝

Great accommodation, very nice cabins, we were in a spa cabin. Great price and fantastic value. The park was very accommodating to our large vehicle and trailer when we came in wanting a cabin for a night. Highly recommended this park if you're staying in Apollo Bay! Thanks again team!
Apollo Bay Holiday Park is fantastic! The facilities are super clean, and the staff are incredibly friendly. Highly recommend for a relaxing stay in the area. We will be back 😊
Amenities clean camp kitchen tops,and the lady at the office Linda is exceptional
Fantastic holiday park. Clean and close to pubs and shopping. Cabins are incredibly neat, clean and comfortable. We'll be back!

Expensive caravan site. The sites were extremely small and we had to move our cars for other vans coming in or ask them to move for us due to limited space. Wouldn’t recommend to any with vans. The amenities were excellent and clean but the park is set up for cabins, not vans. We had to change sites to get the vans in and it only worked as very few other vans there. Had it been full it would have been impossible to get the vans in and out. We only have medium size vans. There were were 4 vans in our group.
Only stayed one night! Bruce was very helpful! Great amenities and very clean!
Nice and quiet  clean park
Friendly and helpful staff, and our cabin was perfect. Clean, tidy and spacious. Would definitely stay here again!

A well laid out park with good sized sites and great amenities. The park is set back a block off the highway so there is no traffic noise. The camp kitchen was well equipped, one of only a few I have seen with a pizza oven. In warmer weather the pool would have been popular . The amenities block was clean and shower cubicles were a good size.
Very clean and tidy caravan park but as with all G'day parks even with the rewards it is average $15.00 more expensive per night than nearly all other caravan parks.

The room was nice enough and had everything we needed. It wasn't the most kid friendly holiday park. The playground wasn't fenced and it was a very small playground. There is also no view of the ocean or anything it's down a side street. Fairly small caravan park was sufficient for a short stay.
The staff were very rude. We complained about the broken stove chimney but they never fixed it. The bunker bed was very dirty,there were no sheets on it.
Perfect location. Spotless, well maintained park. Excellent camp kitchen and beautiful new amenities. So glad we stayed.
unpleasant staff
Pretty good for price, very dated though
This is a gem of a place so clean, camp kitchen also clean location is great from town and beach. This holiday park is awesome 👌🏼👌🏼
Beautiful park well maintained and amazing amenities.

Very accommodating with a late check in time of midnight. Nice and clean facilities and able to park the high wagon no issues next to the cabin we hired. Lovely walk to the beach and into town. Heaps of facilities especially for families. Safe and quiet environment. Was a pleasant experience and will be back.
Lovely pitch, excellent facilities,very clean

This holiday park has been the best presented of all the camps we have stayed at on our travels across NSW, South Australia and Victoria!
Absolutely spotless amenities and the whole camp is in perfect condition. The Toilet Dump Point even has a hot water tap fitted!

Owner Bruce was very welcoming and sorted out my mistake on payment in a very caring and professional attitude.

The camp even has its own resident koalas to entertain the guests!

And our early-morning drive along the coast from Apollo Bay to Airey's Inlet was absolutely spectacular.

One of the tidiest caravan parks we've visited. It's not big but it has everything you need from the well maintained camp kitchen, a nice playground and a nice little swimming pool. We had a site for our caravan which was perfect and overall nice and quiet. Amenities were spotless as was the camp kitchen. The management were helpful and pleasant and the park was well located for our first 4 days on the Great Ocean Walk. Would highly recommend for caravans and campervans!

We rented a site for $140 aud per night

The kitchen utensils as the toaster and the boiler did not work. Bad attitude and service from part of the staff once I told them it was wrong

On top of that, they charge for everything, for example the games to play and the barbecue. For such a big price is surprising they do it. On top of that they only accept $1 coins.

Upgrade for tapping!

Wonderful wend staying with friends at the holiday park. Apollo bay is a beautiful place and we had a great wend till we had to pay the manager Bruce. What an obnoxious, arrogant and rude man. I pity people who have to deal with him on a regular basis. A great park run by a dictator.

While the place was clean & tidy the owner Bruce was not, he ripped us off a night & called us liars. After hearing stories from other holiday makers he is known to abuse some guest's. Would I stay there again, most definitely not nor would I recommend it. The other staff members were good & his wife.

This is by far one of the best holidays parks we have ever been. The pool and playground are small in compared with other parks like in South Australia (Barrosa Valley) however the location is fantastic, it's clean and staff are lovely as well.

Had a powered site, which was great, awesome camp kitchen.
Friendly staff, amazing town.

Limited big van sites but very accommodating.||We arrived late afternoon with a large van & car, ||We found the owner very helpful & accommodating.||On looking around the park, wow wow wow, the grounds immaculate.||The amenities again plenty of space well layed out & extremely clean.||Kids play ground very good & great camp kitchen.|||
Beautiful holiday park. Nice and clean. Amazing staff.
Very clean and maintained to a high standard, manager very friendly and helpful

Incredible holiday park! The cabins were so great and staff were incredible. Our family have stayed here on multiple occasions over the years and will continue to do so! The public toilets are so clean and there are many amenities as well.
Just googled this park to stay there next Friday, Saturday and Sunday booking site said 4 days minimum OK but at $197 a night for a caravan site I don't think so

Excellent service also extremely clean good location very friendly manager top quality park highly recommended
Richard er
Very good very good perfect

We stayed here last night. Bruce was the best host and gave some very good travelling tips.
We highly recommend this caravan park, it is the cleanest and tidiest park we have stayed in.
Comfortable beds and lounge which can sometimes be lacking in caravan parks.
Would loved to have stayed longer but we were passing through
Will definitely return if we are down this way again.
Thanks Bruce 😊

I rarely write reviews but this park is worthy of one. Stayed in many van parks throughout Australia and this one would have to be one of the most clean, well maintained and reasonably priced parks. Amenities and other facilities were exceptionally clean. We had a drive through site.

We stayed here recently on our way up the Great Ocean Road. Location is second to none, and park facilities were thoroughly enjoyed by our group of 9. Despite what previous reviews state about the owners of this park; we found Bruce brilliant to deal with. We had a small hiccup with our room and brought up said issue with Bruce, he was super accommodating and polite in dealing with it. We would definitely stay here again.

Sad and tired are words to come to mind to describe this holiday park. The cabin was dark and gloomy. The stove didn’t work. There were no towels in the room and we had to chase the manager to get some.  We were going out for a walk and he asked us to come back and collect them! The white towels had a greyish tinge. We asked for a hand towel and washer and we were told they don’t supply them. There was also no soap in the bathroom.
Nice tidy cabins, everything you need for a short stay.  Close to the Main Street and in walking distance.  Recommended!

View was magnificent, early morning coffee watching the ocean.|waste water sump was very inconvenient right next to caravan door.|Sites need to be delineated for easy access.|Too few toilets for size of park|Excellent camp kitchen, with good facilities

Apollo Bay Holiday Park is located in a peaceful residential area right at the heart of town. It's just a 5-minute walk to shops and supermarkets on the main street and a quick 3-minute stroll to the beach. The park itself is well-maintained and tidy. While the cabin we stayed in had somewhat dated facilities, they were well-kept and spotlessly clean. The only frustration was that the gas stove and oven in our cabin seemed tricky to light. Despite this minor hiccup, the overall experience was pleasant, and the convenience of the location made our stay enjoyable.

Immaculate little caravan park with playground and games room for children. Amenities were very clean. Showers had glass doors, timber slatted mat and bathmat with lots of hot water....heavenly! 5mins drive into township. Foodworks being the larger of the two supermarkets with more stock. Had fish and chips at the Boat harbour and were visited by a big seal. Very special.
Highly recommend this park and township for a stopover.

Best Holiday Park ever!
Hat die perfekte Lage in Apollo Bay nah am Meer und zur Stadt. Der Park hat alles zu bieten wie Gamingräume, Campkitchen und Duschräume die alle Super sauber und modern sind.
Das Personal ist immer hilfsbereit und super nett gewesen, was unser Erlebnis in Apollo Bay einfach perfekt gemacht hat.
Immer wieder gerne!

We had our own van, facilities lovely and clean, friendly staff. Friends stayed in cabins,which were very clean and well set out.
Playground for kids and a swimming pool too.
Drive past it 5 days a week looks great
Very nice clean tidy park.  The amenities were excellent. Lovely managers.

Very close to the centre of Apollo bay. Room was comfortable and clean. Play area, pool and gaming area were kept clean. Only cons were TV remote was found broken and gas stove spark plug was found damaged and hence, had to use match sticks.
